title: Market Brief — Wednesday, April 1, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 19% (51/264), H200 at 20% (66/323), and B200 is loosest at 33% (119/366) as new supply trickles in. The headline move: H100 spot crashed 21.3% overnight to $1.47/hr, signaling either a demand air pocket or inventory dump despite tight supply.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 19% (51/264), H200 at 20% (66/323), and B200 is loosest at 33% (119/366) as new supply trickles in. The headline move: H100 spot crashed 21.3% overnight to $1.47/hr, signaling either a demand air pocket or inventory dump despite tight supply.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), self-hosting Llama 70B on H100 runs $5.36/M tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $4.98 per million tokens, pricing that only makes sense as market-share warfare. Medium-tier API providers are underwater across all GPU classes, losing 43-82% on every token served. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when GPT-5.4 delivers frontier capability at $15/M, a 5x premium begging to compress. DeepSeek V3.2 and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at sub-$0.60/M output are undervalued for production workloads; rotate medium-tier API spend into self-hosted open-source on B200s where 33% availability gives you actual procurement options.
bullets: 19% of H100 GPUs are available, well inside the severe-shortage-to-scarce zone at just 51 free units.
33% B200 availability with 119 free GPUs makes it the only class offering realistic procurement flexibility today.
$1.47/hr H100 spot is down 21.3% day-over-day, the sharpest single-day GPU price drop in the dataset.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly $4.98 below TCO-adjusted production cost per million tokens.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 for frontier-adjacent reasoning.
